activities_projects.title: Chlorophyll and Sunlight
activities_projects.objective: To show that sunlight is necessary for photosynthesis
steps: Take a healthy potted plant and keep it in the dark for 24 hours
Cover a part of one leaf with black paper using clips
Keep the plant in sunlight for 6 hours
Remove the leaf and test it for starch using iodine solution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Observes that starch is only formed in the parts of the leaf exposed to sunlight
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the blue-black color change location (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water Transport in Stems
activities_projects.objective: Observe how water moves upward through the stem
materials: A white flower with a stem (like a balsam or carnation)
steps: Fill a glass halfway with water and add a few drops of red ink
Cut the base of the flower stem and place it in the colored water
Leave it undisturbed for a few hours or overnight
Observe the change in the color of the flower petals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the upward movement of water through xylem vessels
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of how the color reached the petals (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Transpiration in Plants
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate that plants release water vapor into the air
steps: Tie a polythene bag around a leafy branch of a healthy potted plant
Keep the plant in the sun for 1-2 hours
Observe the inner surface of the bag for water droplets
Compare with an empty bag tied to a stick nearby
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies transpiration as the process of water loss from leaves
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful observation and recording of water droplets (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Stomata Observation
activities_projects.objective: Identify the tiny pores on the leaf surface
materials: A fleshy leaf (like Tradescantia or Lily)
steps: Peel off a thin layer from the underside of the leaf
Place the peel on a flat surface
Examine the peel closely using a magnifying glass
Look for tiny dots or openings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes stomata as the sites for gas exchange and transpiration
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct description of the appearance of leaf pores (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Root Absorption Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate that roots absorb water for the plant
steps: Fill both test tubes with equal amounts of water
Place a small plant in Test Tube A with its roots submerged
Add a layer of oil in both tubes to prevent evaporation
Mark the water level and check after 24 hours
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Observes that the water level drops only in the tube with the plant
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate marking and explanation of the water level change (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Starch in Leaves
activities_projects.objective: Test different leaves for the presence of stored food
steps: Boil the leaf in water for a few minutes to soften it
Place the leaf in a tube of alcohol and heat it in a water bath to remove chlorophyll
Wash the leaf in cold water
Add a few drops of iodine and observe the color change
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Confirms that plants store food in the form of starch
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of the chemical test for starch (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Growing Plants from Scraps
activities_projects.objective: Observe vegetative propagation in common vegetables
steps: Cut a piece of potato containing at least one 'eye'
Plant it in moist soil
Place a money plant cutting in a glass of water
Observe the growth of new roots and shoots over two weeks
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that plants can reproduce through parts other than seeds
activities_projects.evaluation: Weekly log of growth observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Autotroph vs. Heterotroph Chart
activities_projects.objective: Classify organisms based on their mode of nutrition
materials: Pictures of various plants and animals
steps: Divide the chart into two columns: Autotrophs and Heterotrophs
Categorize pictures of green plants, mushrooms, and animals
Write one sentence on why green plants are called producers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between organisms that make food and those that depend on others
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of at least 10 organisms (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Saprotrophic Observation
activities_projects.objective: Observe how fungi obtain nutrition from dead matter
steps: Moisten a slice of bread with a little water
Keep it in a warm, dark place inside a closed container
Observe the bread for 3-4 days for any fluffy growth
Use a magnifying glass to see the thread-like structures
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies fungi as saprotrophs that feed on decaying matter
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of the fungal growth and its source of nutrition (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Oxygen Bubbles Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Observe plants giving out oxygen during the day
steps: Place the plant in a beaker filled with water
Invert a funnel over the plant
Keep the setup in bright sunlight
Observe the tiny bubbles rising from the plant surface
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates that oxygen is a byproduct of photosynthesis
activities_projects.evaluation: Connection between light and bubble production (10 marks)
